title: Solana Sign
summary: Solana Sign is a wallet-native document-signing surface for Solana users. Arcium includes it in consumer-app ecosystem coverage, but the public Solana Sign materials I reviewed do not document a specific Arcium-powered feature.
description:
Solana Sign keeps its value proposition simple: sign documents using Solana wallets. That makes it a useful Atlas reference point because document workflows, identity proofs, and selective disclosure are exactly the kinds of surfaces where encrypted computation could become product-relevant.

Arcium includes Solana Sign in the encrypted consumer-app ecosystem. That is enough for Atlas inclusion, but I did not find a current Solana Sign page documenting a specific Arcium-powered module.

bodySections.title: Consumer_Surface
bodySections.body: Solana Sign is a good example of a lightweight but consequential consumer utility. Wallet-based document signing sits at the edge of identity, records, attestations, and private coordination, which gives the project clear relevance even without a currently documented Arcium module.

bodySections.title: Arcium_Relevance
bodySections.body: Arcium lists Solana Sign among consumer-app ecosystem partners. That supports inclusion in Atlas, but not a confirmed-integration label from current public materials.

statusNote: Arcium includes Solana Sign in its consumer-app ecosystem coverage, but I did not find a current Solana Sign page documenting a specific Arcium-powered feature.

sources.label: Solana Sign Website
sources.note: Official site positioning Solana Sign around document signing with Solana wallets.

sources.label: Arcium Encrypted Ecosystem
sources.note: Arcium lists Solana Sign among consumer-app ecosystem partners.
